title: ITI Jobs 2026 – Junior Engineer, Tracer and More
applyMode: Online only via www.ossc.gov.in. No Physical copy/Hard copy of Online Application Form to be submitted.
shortInfo: OSSC CTSRE Recruitment 2026 notification is out to fill 646 Junior Engineer, Tracer and More vacancies across Odisha. Candidates with Diploma/Degree, ITI, 12TH Can Apply Online. The online application opens on 30-05-2026, and closes on 29-06-2026. The candidate shall apply online through OSSC website, ossc.gov.in/.

basic_details.job_title: OSSC CTSRE Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 646 Junior Engineer, Tracer and More Posts
basic_details.application_mode: Online only via www.ossc.gov.in. No Physical copy/Hard copy of Online Application Form to be submitted.
basic_details.short_information: OSSC CTSRE Recruitment 2026 notification is out to fill 646 Junior Engineer, Tracer and More vacancies across Odisha. Candidates with Diploma/Degree, ITI, 12TH Can Apply Online. The online application opens on 30-05-2026, and closes on 29-06-2026. The candidate shall apply online through OSSC website, ossc.gov.in/.

application_fee.details: Candidates are required to pay Examination fees through online mode only via the Odisha Treasury portal linked with the online application form. The examination fee paid shall be refunded to candidates who actually appear in Stage-I (Preliminary Examination). Applications without fee payment (except SC/ST/PwD candidates) will be treated as incomplete and liable for rejection. All candidates must mention their bank account details in the online application form to receive the refund.

age_limit.details: Candidates must be a minimum of 21 years and maximum of 42 years as on 01-01-2025, i.e., candidates must not have been born earlier than 2nd January 1983 and not later than 1st January 2004 (both days inclusive). Maximum age indicated is for Unreserved candidates. Relaxation in upper age limit is applicable as per Government of Odisha guidelines.

qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the last date of submission of Online Application Form. Only Indian Citizens are eligible to apply. Candidates must be able to read, write and speak Odia fluently and must have passed Middle School Examination with Odia as a language subject, or HSC with Odia as medium, or passed Odia as language subject in Class-VII or above from a school recognised by Govt. of Odisha/Central Govt., or passed a test in Odia at Middle English School standard conducted by School and Mass Education Department.

salary_details.pay_scale: Level-9, Cell-1 / Level-8, Cell-1 / Level-5, Cell-1 of ORSP, 2017 (as applicable per post)
salary_details.salary: Level-9, Cell-1 / Level-8, Cell-1 / Level-5, Cell-1 of ORSP, 2017 (as applicable per post)

exam_pattern.subject: One Paper: Arithmetic (10th standard), Data Interpretation (Chart, Graph, Table, Data Sufficiency – 10th standard), Logical Reasoning and Analytical Ability, General Mental Ability, Current Events of National and International Importance, Computer/Internet Awareness
Technical Paper (syllabus as per Annexure-D of notification; Technical Paper for Junior Draughtsman (Civil) and Tracer will be the same)
exam_pattern.marks: Different papers for different posts. Candidates numbering 1.5 times the advertised vacancies (post-wise and category-wise) shall be shortlisted for Certificate Verification. Negative marking: 1/4 of total mark (4 options) or 1/3 of total mark (3 options)

physical_eligibility.running: (i) High School Certificate or equivalent; (ii) NTC (Draughtsman Civil) from a recognised institution; (iii) Certificate towards Basic Computer Skill; (iv) Certificate towards Auto CAD Course from a recognised institution

how_to_apply: Admission Letter (downloaded from www.ossc.gov.in) along with valid photo identity proof in original and photocopy
Original academic certificates and marksheets (relevant Diploma/Degree/Certificate onwards)
Caste Certificate in the prescribed Govt. of Odisha format (for SEBC/SC/ST candidates)
Disability Certificate / UDID Card issued by competent Medical Authority (for PwD candidates) — scanned copy of original to be uploaded at application stage
Sports ID Card issued by Director of Sports & Youth Service Department, Govt. of Odisha (for Sports Person candidates)
No Objection Certificate (NOC) from competent controlling authority (for Government servants / PSU employees)
Any other documents as intimated in the Admission Letter for Certificate Verification (details in Annexure-B of notification)
important_instructions: The recruitment shall be guided by "Combined Technical Services Recruitment Examination Rules-2022" as amended from time to time.
Candidates must possess a valid email ID and mobile number while applying and keep them active till completion of the recruitment process to receive important messages from the Commission.

The appointment will be only against one of the posts carrying the corresponding pay as indicated in the vacancy table; candidates applying for more than one post/service must give option/preference for the post/service in case they qualify.

The Commission will adopt a "Normalisation Formula" published vide Notice No.2444/OSSC dated 02.09.2021 for processing results if any examination is conducted through CBRE/OMR mode in multiple batches using different sets of question papers.
No Admission Letter for recruitment at any stage shall be sent by post; candidates must download from www.ossc.gov.in regularly for updates.
Candidates are advised to submit the Online Application Form well in advance without waiting for the closing date to avoid last-hour rush.
All posts are State Cadre posts; the candidate on selection may be asked to serve anywhere in Odisha.
OBC (Creamy Layer) candidates are not entitled to SEBC reservation; they must apply under UR category.
PwD candidates must ensure they possess a permanent disability certificate issued online and upload the scanned copy of the original (not photocopy) of the Disability Certificate/UDID Card.
There shall be no provision for re-evaluation/re-checking of scores; no correspondence in this regard shall be entertained.
Mobile phones, Bluetooth devices, spy cameras, smartwatches and any other electronic gadgets are strictly prohibited inside the examination hall.
In case of tie in scores, merit will be decided by: (i) Marks in Preliminary Examination, (ii) Date of Birth with older candidate placed higher, and (iii) Alphabetical order of names.

The Commission's decision in all matters relating to eligibility, acceptance/rejection of applications, mode of selection, conduct of examination, allotment of examination centres and preparation of merit list shall be final and binding.
Any complaint on the conduct of the examination must be sent to ossc.od@nic.in within 05 (five) days of completion of examination.
Canvassing in any form will be a disqualification.

faq.question: What is OSSC CTSRE 2025 Recruitment?
faq.answer: OSSC CTSRE 2025 is a Combined Technical Services Recruitment Examination conducted by the Odisha Staff Selection Commission to fill 646 Group-B and Group-C technical posts across various departments.

faq.question: How many vacancies are available in OSSC CTSRE 2025?
faq.answer: A total of 646 vacancies have been announced, mainly for Junior Engineer (Civil) along with posts like Architectural Assistant, Cameraman, and others.

faq.question: What is the last date to apply for OSSC CTSRE 2025?
faq.answer: The last date for online registration and fee payment is 29 June 2026, while the final submission deadline is 2 July 2026.

faq.question: What is the eligibility for OSSC CTSRE 2025?
faq.answer: Candidates must have a Diploma/Degree in relevant fields (like Civil Engineering, Architecture, or Media-related disciplines) and must be able to read, write, and speak Odia.

faq.question: What is the age limit for OSSC CTSRE 2025?
faq.answer: Applicants must be between 21 to 42 years as on 01 January 2025, with age relaxation applicable for reserved categories as per Odisha government rules.

faq.question: What is the selection process for OSSC CTSRE 2025?
faq.answer: The selection process includes:Preliminary Examination (Objective)Main Written Examination (Technical)Certificate Verification

faq.question: Is there any application fee for OSSC CTSRE 2025?
faq.answer: Yes, ₹500 for General/SEBC candidates (refundable after appearing in prelims), while SC/ST/PwD candidates are exempted.

faq.question: What is the exam pattern of OSSC CTSRE 2025?
faq.answer: The prelim exam consists of 150 MCQs (150 marks), and the mains includes a 200-mark technical paper. Negative marking applies in both stages.

faq.question: Can candidates from other states apply for OSSC CTSRE 2025?
faq.answer: Yes, but only Indian citizens are eligible. However, candidates must fulfill the Odia language requirement to qualify.

faq.question: How to apply for OSSC CTSRE 2025 online?
faq.answer: Candidates can apply online through the official website ossc.gov.in by completing registration, filling out the application form, uploading documents, and paying the fee.
